routingCapabilities.evidence: argus-launch verifies an externally signed, short-lived exact launch authorization, binds it to one process tree with a one-shot random capability whose digest is signed, and starts Odysseus with Claude's native --max-turns 96, the reviewed opus/max controller baseline, an environment allowlist, and an OS filesystem sandbox whose only writable root is the alias-free artifact boundary. Direct or file-only replayed /argus:run is blocked by preflight.

codex.sandboxPolicy: read-only when the capability contract has no Write tool; workspace-write otherwise
codex.doctrinePolicy: embed only capability-selected doctrine profiles; load hash-bound technique catalogs lazily after surface inventory, with full-catalog fallback
codex.toolPolicy: require contract-equivalent runtime capabilities and return CAPABILITY_GAP when an assigned capability is unavailable

routingCapabilities.evidence: Codex TOML enforces the reviewed baseline model and reasoning effort. The installed CLI has no native hard turn cap, so every Codex route fails closed with CAPABILITY_DRIFT; a signed claim cannot replace missing runtime enforcement.

explicitDifferences.codex: runtime-provided tools with provenance and fail-closed fallback
explicitDifferences.reason: Claude and Codex expose different tool vocabularies

explicitDifferences.codex: Codex collaboration tools when provided, otherwise an executable parent-session plan

explicitDifferences.claude: capability-selected plugin skills preloaded from the matrix
explicitDifferences.codex: the same selected profiles embedded into developer_instructions
explicitDifferences.reason: standalone Codex custom agents do not load Claude plugin skills

explicitDifferences.claude: CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT and argus-assets are installed with the plugin
explicitDifferences.codex: use them only when the parent supplies the installed plugin; otherwise return CAPABILITY_GAP
explicitDifferences.reason: Codex agents are installed as standalone TOML files
